summary: Iteration 20 - Phase A Procurement Cycle Backbone (PR -> RFQ -> PO -> GRN). Backend pytest 17/17 PASS (test_iteration20_procurement.py). Frontend Playwright walkthrough as admin@erp.com verified end-to-end: /app/procurement-dashboard renders procurement-dashboard with 8 KPI tiles (pd-tile-*) + 4 quick-action buttons (pd-quick-*); /app/purchase-requisitions renders prs-page with prs-add button, dialog has pr-priority dropdown + pr-submit-now checkbox + pr-add-item + pr-save, list shows 5 pr-row-* with 4 pr-approval-* links + 2 pr-submit-* buttons; /app/rfqs renders rfqs-page with rfqs-add dialog containing rfq-pr-select (approved-only - currently empty since the only approved PR has been converted), rfq-vendor-chips with 5 rfq-vendor-{vid} chips, rfq-due-date, rfq-notes, rfq-save; RFQ list shows 2 rfq-row-* with rfq-respond + rfq-compare buttons each; /app/grn renders grn-page with grn-add dialog including grn-po-select that correctly filters out 'received'/'cancelled' POs (5 options including PO-2026-0001 and PO-2026-0002), grn-save; sidebar has all 4 entries (nav-prs, nav-rfqs, nav-grn, nav-procurement-dashboard); /app/modules/procurement workspace shows 7 KPI tiles (Vendor Base, PR Pending, RFQ Open, Pending POs, GRN Total, Approved Vendors, Total POs) + 7 module tiles (Procurement Dashboard, Purchase Requisitions, RFQs, Purchase Orders, GRN, Vendor Master, Approvals). Backend behaviour confirmed: POST /prs auto-numbers PR-YYYY-#### and with submit_for_approval=true auto-creates a 5-step purchase_requisition approval; POST /prs/{id}/submit promotes draft/rejected; 5 approve actions flip pr.status to approved; reject sets reject_reason; PUT blocked once approved; RFQ requires approved PR (400 otherwise); responses bump status to under_evaluation; comparative returns rows sorted by landed_value=rate*total_qty; select-vendor validates vendor membership; convert-to-po creates PO-YYYY-#### linked to RFQ+PR, flips rfq.status='converted_to_po' and pr.status='po_generated'; GRN line math (total_accepted=120, total_rejected=10) yields status='partial_accepted', inventory.quantity +70 for the line carrying item_id, inventory_transactions row written, po.status flipped to partially_received; DELETE /grns/{id} reverses inventory inward (-70); dashboard returns all 11 KPIs incl. avg_cycle_days; purchase_officer can read /procurement/prs (RBAC wildcard for read).

issues: PR dropdown only shows literal '— pick a PR —' when no PR is in 'approved' state. Consider rendering an inline hint like 'No approved PRs yet - approve a PR first' so the user understands why the dropdown is empty rather than thinking the modal is broken.

PO options show amount as ₹ 0 for several rows (PO-2026-003/004/005) because legacy POs (created before the RFQ-conversion flow) have amount=0/null. The dropdown still shows them as eligible. Consider visually de-emphasising or hiding amount when null.

Status badge tone is uniform across all states (no colour differentiation between draft/pending_approval/approved/rejected/po_generated beyond the small Badge variant). At a glance it is hard to tell a draft from an approved PR without reading the text.

action_items: (LOW) Show an inline empty-state in the RFQ create dialog when no PRs are in 'approved' status so the user knows the dropdown is intentionally empty.
(LOW) GRN PO dropdown lists legacy POs with amount=0; consider hiding amount or adding a 'Legacy' badge for clarity.
(LOW) Colour-code PR status badges (e.g., draft=secondary, pending_approval=warning, approved=success, rejected=destructive, po_generated=info) for at-a-glance scanning.
(LOW) Confirm intent: backend procurement_router.create_pr returns 200, but a POST creating a resource is typically 201 — align with the rest of the codebase if 201 is the convention.
critical_code_review_comments: procurement_router.convert_rfq_to_po blindly multiplies rate * sum(item.quantity). When PR items have heterogeneous units (e.g., 100 Kg of steel + 50 Nos of bolts), summing the quantities is meaningless — the resulting amount is wrong by design. A proper landed-value calc must store per-item rates (or at least per-UoM rates), or restrict comparative/conversion to a single-line PR.
procurement_router.comparative_statement uses the same total_qty across items: landed_value = rate * sum(items.quantity) — same UoM bug as above; comparative ranking is unreliable for multi-item PRs.

procurement_router.create_grn allows total_recv > po.qty without any check (over-receipt). Add an assertion that accepted_qty <= remaining_qty per line, otherwise GRN can inflate inventory beyond what was ordered.

procurement_router.delete_grn always reverses inventory regardless of whether downstream stock has already been issued out — if a project has consumed the inward stock and the GRN is then deleted, inventory.quantity goes negative. Consider blocking delete once any outward txn references the GRN, or at least logging a warning.

procurement_router.create_grn flips po.status to 'received' on the FIRST GRN even if accepted < ordered (the current logic only marks it partial when ANY line has inspection_status='partial_accepted' or 'rejected'). A GRN that fully accepts 50 of 100 ordered units (received_qty < ordered_qty but inspection_status='approved') will silently close the PO. Compare cumulative accepted across all GRNs to ordered qty before setting 'received'.

procurement_router.create_pr stores doc['requested_by_id']=user['id'] but the 'submit' endpoint never validates that only the requester (or higher role) can re-submit a rejected PR — anyone with purchase_requisitions:write can submit any PR.

procurement_router.submit_pr does NOT soft-delete or mark the previous (rejected) approval; /app/approvals will list the stale rejected doc alongside the new pending one. Same issue flagged for clients_router in iter-19 — fix consistently.

approval_engine.purchase_requisition chain hardcodes 5 roles (Dept Head → PM → Procurement Head → Finance → Director). If a tenant lacks any one role, the chain stalls forever. Add a fall-through to super_admin / director, or expose a config table.

RFQ status='approved' (after select-vendor) is overloaded — both 'vendor selected, not yet converted' and 'final, post-PO' share the same string. Use a clearer state like 'vendor_selected' to avoid confusion with the PO-creation step.

PurchaseRequisitions.jsx, Rfqs.jsx, Grn.jsx, ProcurementDashboard.jsx — none of them surface backend error toasts on save/convert/delete failures (no try/catch reporting). A 400 from the API will silently fail in the UI; add a toast on .catch.

Rfqs.jsx vendor multi-select uses 'rfq-vendor-{vendor.id}' as testid on the chip button but the parent container also uses 'rfq-vendor-chips' — both share the 'rfq-vendor-' prefix, causing query_selector_all('[data-testid^="rfq-vendor-"]') to include the container. Rename the container to 'rfq-vendor-list' to avoid clashes in tests.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on Phase A Procurement testids - procurement-dashboard with 8 tiles + 4 quick actions, prs-page + prs-add + dialog + 5 rows, rfqs-page + rfqs-add + dialog with PR/vendor/due/notes + 2 RFQ rows with respond/compare buttons, grn-page + grn-add + PO filtering + 1 GRN row, all 4 sidebar nav entries present, procurement workspace with 7 KPIs + 7 module tiles.

test_credentials: admin@erp.com / Admin@123 (super_admin - bypasses approval steps, used for E2E). purchase@erp.com / Purchase@123 verified read-only RBAC.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create multiple TEST_pr_item_<ts> / TEST_steel_<ts> / TEST_bolts_<ts> / TEST_rej_<ts> PRs, one TEST_PROC_ITEM_<ts> inventory item (if needed), and TEST_RFQ + a GRN derived from them. PRs, RFQs, the resulting PO and approval docs are NOT cleaned up (no DELETE exposed past 'approved'/'po_generated'). They will accumulate as historical records. GRN created by the test is deleted in test_delete_grn_reverses_inventory.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase A Procurement testids: PR list - prs-page, prs-add, prs-search, pr-row-{id}, pr-submit-{id} (only on draft/rejected), pr-approval-{id} (when approval_id present), pr-rfq-{id} (only when status=approved), pr-delete-{id}. PR dialog - pr-priority, pr-add-item, pr-item-{idx}, pr-remove-item-{idx}, pr-submit-now (checkbox), pr-save. RFQ - rfqs-page, rfqs-add, rfq-row-{id}, rfq-respond-{id}, rfq-compare-{id}, rfq-convert-{id} (only when selected_vendor_id set), rfq-po-link-{id}. RFQ dialog - rfq-pr-select (approved-only), rfq-vendor-chips container, rfq-vendor-{vid} chip buttons (multi-select), rfq-due-date, rfq-notes, rfq-save. RFQ response dialog - rfq-respond-row-{vid}, rfq-save-{vid}. RFQ compare dialog - rfq-compare-row-{vid}, rfq-select-{vid}. GRN - grn-page, grn-add, grn-row-{id}, grn-delete-{id}. GRN dialog - grn-po-select (filters out received/cancelled), grn-store, grn-date, grn-line-{i}, grn-remarks, grn-save. Dashboard - procurement-dashboard, pd-tile-* (8 tiles), pd-quick-* (4 buttons). Sidebar - nav-prs, nav-rfqs, nav-grn, nav-procurement-dashboard. Endpoints: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/procurement/prs, POST /api/procurement/prs/{id}/submit, GET/POST /api/procurement/rfqs, POST /api/procurement/rfqs/{id}/respond, GET /api/procurement/rfqs/{id}/comparative, POST /api/procurement/rfqs/{id}/select-vendor, POST /api/procurement/rfqs/{id}/convert-to-po, GET/POST/DELETE /api/procurement/grns, GET /api/procurement/dashboard. The status='approved' overloading in RFQ (means vendor-selected) might confuse future tests. Run: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration20_procurement.py -v
rca of the issue: Not a bug fix - new module verification. Phase A delivers the full Procurement Cycle (PR creation with 5-step approval, RFQ multi-vendor with comparative statement and vendor selection, auto PO conversion, GRN with line-level accept/reject + inventory inward, dashboard with cycle-time KPIs and RBAC). All 17 backend tests pass against the production preview URL; all 4 frontend pages render and their critical testids are present; all 4 sidebar entries gate correctly; the procurement workspace at /modules/procurement reflects the new 7 KPIs + 7 module tiles. The 8 critical code-review concerns flagged are pre-emptive (UoM-aware landed cost, over-receipt guard, GRN reversal safety, RFQ status taxonomy, approval-chain fallback, UI error toasts) - none block Phase A acceptance but should be addressed before Phase B.
